item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a strangely barebones skip, presented in a very matter-of-fact and stilted manner. There's just something slightly off, tone-wise, about the writing, and it overall comes off dry. That said, I'm somewhat tickled a research team would make a D-Class walk two weeks to get to Denver. I'm not sure that's going to fit a lot of people's headcanons, but, well, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers sorta?
– hide block

As the logs went on I had a building suspicion that Gerdinel might have some further significance. Especially by the penultimate entry where they were the only staff mentioned by name. The focus on that particular researcher's actions seemed like it was leading somewhere, and I was a bit surprised to see essentially identical behavior in the final log.

Idle musing aside, this was a nice somber piece that held my attention throughout and left me wanting to know more. A good feeling to leave on, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
This was really nice and interesting. As Modern_Erasmus has already mentioned, it's really nice to see SCP staff have respect for D-class personnel.

Also I really liked how you implied some explanations of the phenomenon without outright saying anything :) especially the attempted suicide one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor spelling whatsit: Poloroid should be capitalized if it is specifically meant to refer to the brand, or should be changed to "instant film" if it is meant to be generic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
iWhat I got was that someone died at each of the locations in the photo's, and wanted a gift. The person who died at the ice caves was lonely, so the subject tried to kill himself to join them.

In any case, this is a great SCP.
It feels very… "realistically simple", if that makes any sense. It leaves things open to interpretation without being too minimalist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the haiku catch! Luckily that one is easy to shorten.

I do have reasoning behind the the last log and for the husband, but if it isn't apparent then that's something I should look into strengthening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoy how human and sympathetic the Foundation is here. Cold not cruel is a motto that all too often writers use to justify outright cruelty, and the simple act of concern for the welfare of a couple of D-class feels refreshing because of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the last test log, the haiku is 5-7-6 instead of 5-7-5, is that intentional? Aside from that, I feel this SCP tells you enough to let you figure out what's going on but doesn't beat you over the head with it, which is nice. Definitely not bad for a second SCP, but the sudden murder/suicide feels a bit jarring considering the rest of the article seems fairly well thought-out, whereas it's not really suggested or explained why he would do such a thing. I might be missing something but the husband just feels like he's there to fill a role and little else. Hesitant +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know so much about removing the recovery logs, but I do agree that the blunt statements could be removed. So I went ahead and did that. Thanks for the suggestions!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still enjoy the premise, but not the execution. In retrospect, I think the content of the logs would better geared towards focusing on building a mystery with each instance, that is, inducing the requested items without blunt statements from the Ds, and allowing readers to piece together everything that way. You could probably even make it work without the exposition in the recovery log.

I think it's leaning too far into detailing the minutiae of how strong the anomalous compulsion is, to the detriment of the more interesting aspects of the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I swear I fixed these things during the last round of sandbox edits… but it appears they did not save correctly or something. Thanks again for catching that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Couple of minor things.

From test log two: When asked about the pail,

Not sure what pail is referring to.

From log 05: Subject walks to Highway I-25 and follows it in the direction of Colorado. The entire research team follows in cars approximately 20 meters behind the subject

A car full of people following someone walking down the highway? So the car was on I-25 doing 2mph?

From log 09: Subject no longer has useful Subject begins pacing, possibly to relieve stress.
And log 12: Assist the subject reach his destination by any means necessary.

Looks like you accidentally a word.

item.title: SCP-3650
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone! Second SCP now, and hopefully I'm getting the hang of this.

I want to thank Leveritas, Shaggydredlocks, Blackfluid (or Hyperfluid, whichever his name actually is) and toastedraptor for critiques and feedback.

All pictures were taken and modified by me.

I hope you enjoyed!

If you want to read more of my work, you can find it here.
